name: Tortilla Crust Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 whole wheat tortillas, 8 1/2 inch diameter (Garden of Eatin' is 4gms fat per tortilla, larger/smaller tortillas are okay, and fat free might work. GofE ingredients are organically grown whole wheat flour, water, aluminum-free baking powder, soybean oil, and sea salt, 4 gms fat and 130 calories each).
4 Granny Smith apples (quartered, cored and sliced twice more per quarter to get 12 slices per apple; halve each of these slices crosswise to make the apple bits match the berry sizes better)
12 oz. berries (e.g., fresh cranberries, defrosted frozen raspberries)
1/2 cup + 1 to 2 Tbsp sugar or Sucanat
recipeInstructions:
1. Boil the cranberries in water for about 10 minutes until they pop.
2. Strain and reserve the liquid.
3. Simmer the apples with reserved liquid for about 5 minutes until they start to cook.
4. Mix sugar, cornstarch dissolved in cool or lukewarm liquid, berries, and any optional ingredients (e.g., lemon zest, kirsch liquor, currants).
5. Cook the berry mixture over medium heat until it thickens.
6. Assemble the pie by placing one tortilla layer in a 9-inch pie pan, pouring in the heated fruit mixture, then adding a second tortilla layer and pressing down the edges to seal.
7. Optionally brush crust with sugar water solution (2:1 concentration) and sprinkle with sugar or sugar/cinnamon mix.
8. Bake at 400°F for 15 minutes until the crust is crisp.
9. Let cool at room temperature for at least 1 hour before serving, or serve hot.
